name: Robinson & Henry P.C.
description_short: Colorado-based law firm offering bankruptcy representation alongside family law, litigation, criminal defense, and estate planning. Award-winning firm with 23,100+ clients served.
description_long:
Robinson & Henry P.C. is a Colorado legal firm headquartered in Denver with multiple office locations throughout the state. Founded and operating as a full-service law practice, the firm has built a reputation as an award-winning provider of legal services across multiple practice areas. The firm was named a 2025 Best Company to Work For by U.S. News & World Report and recognized as a Top Law Firm in Denver Area by Denver Business Journal.

The firm offers bankruptcy representation as one of 12+ distinct practice areas, alongside family law (their largest practice), business representation, criminal defense, employment law, estate planning and elder law, insurance claim denials, intellectual property, litigation, military law, personal injury and car accidents, probate, and real estate law. For bankruptcy specifically, the firm provides representation for individuals and businesses navigating Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 filings, though detailed bankruptcy-specific service descriptions are not prominently featured on their website.

Robinson & Henry distinguishes itself through its scale (23,100+ documented clients), institutional recognition from major business publications, diverse practice areas allowing for comprehensive legal guidance, and a substantial attorney roster with multiple partners specializing in different practice areas. The firm emphasizes accessibility through multiple consultation methods (chat, phone, online scheduling) and maintains active content marketing with recent articles on Colorado-specific legal issues including debt after death, conservatorship abuse, and property disputes.

However, as a full-service firm, bankruptcy is one of many practice areas rather than a specialization. The website provides minimal specific information about bankruptcy services, fees, Chapter 7 vs. Chapter 13 distinctions, or bankruptcy outcomes. Prospective clients seeking focused bankruptcy expertise may find more detailed information from dedicated bankruptcy specialists, though Robinson & Henry's breadth and institutional resources may benefit complex cases requiring integration with family law, business, or estate planning issues.
pros: 2025 Best Company to Work For designation by U.S. News & World Report indicates institutional stability and quality workplace standards
23,100+ clients served demonstrates significant market presence and consumer trust in Colorado
Multiple practice areas allow integrated representation for clients with complex needs (e.g., bankruptcy combined with family law or business issues)
Multiple office locations throughout Colorado provide geographic accessibility
Flexible consultation access through chat, phone, and online scheduling
Substantial attorney roster with multiple partners across practice areas
Active thought leadership with recent articles on Colorado-specific legal issues
cons: Bankruptcy is one of 12+ practice areas rather than a core specialization, suggesting less focused expertise than dedicated bankruptcy firms
Website provides minimal specific information about bankruptcy services, fee structures, or filing outcomes
No detailed distinction or educational content between Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 options on the site
Heavy emphasis on family law in attorney roster and marketing may indicate disproportionate focus compared to bankruptcy practice
As a larger firm, individual client attention and cost may be higher than boutique bankruptcy specialists
best_for: Colorado residents with bankruptcy needs integrated with family law issues (divorce, child support, asset division)
Business owners facing bankruptcy while requiring concurrent business representation or employment law counsel
Clients seeking comprehensive legal services from a stable, institutionally recognized firm with multiple office locations
Individuals whose bankruptcy case involves estate planning, probate, or complex asset/property considerations
diagnosis: Robinson & Henry P.C. is best for Colorado residents requiring bankruptcy representation who also need concurrent services in family law, business law, or estate planning, or who value institutional stability and geographic accessibility. The primary caveat is that bankruptcy is one of many practice areas rather than a specialization—clients seeking specialized bankruptcy expertise with detailed outcome data and transparent fee structures may benefit from consulting a dedicated bankruptcy firm first.
